description:
You are a performance engineer who just received two freshly-built application drops that you want to profile under a common “tools” area.  
The build script already unpacked the drops into your home tree at:

• /home/user/apps/appAlpha-1.2/bin/alpha  
• /home/user/apps/appBeta-2.0/bin/beta  

Each directory also contains a “perfdata” folder that the applications will fill with performance counters once they run:

• /home/user/apps/appAlpha-1.2/perfdata/  
• /home/user/apps/appBeta-2.0/perfdata/  

Your objective is to prepare a profiling workspace entirely through symbolic links so that when the application binaries are updated in future the workspace can be switched over by simply repointing the links.

Required end-state
1. Create the directory /home/user/profile_workspace/ if it does not already exist.  
2. Under /home/user/profile_workspace/ create exactly two sub-directories named alpha and beta (lower-case).  
3. Inside /home/user/profile_workspace/alpha create two symbolic links:  
   • run  →  /home/user/apps/appAlpha-1.2/bin/alpha  
   • data →  /home/user/apps/appAlpha-1.2/perfdata  
   Inside /home/user/profile_workspace/beta create two symbolic links:  
   • run  →  /home/user/apps/appBeta-2.0/bin/beta  
   • data →  /home/user/apps/appBeta-2.0/perfdata  

   All links must be relative **paths** (so that the workspace remains relocatable) rather than absolute paths.  
4. Produce a tab-separated audit log named /home/user/symlink_audit.log with one header line followed by one line per symbolic link created (total 5 lines).  
   The log must follow this exact TSV schema (including header names, single TAB characters between fields, and the literal strings “VALID” or “BROKEN” in the last column):  

   link_path<TAB>points_to<TAB>state  

   For example, the first two data lines of a correct log would look like:  
   /home/user/profile_workspace/alpha/run<TAB>../../apps/appAlpha-1.2/bin/alpha<TAB>VALID  
   /home/user/profile_workspace/alpha/data<TAB>../../apps/appAlpha-1.2/perfdata<TAB>VALID  

   A link is considered VALID only if the path it resolves to actually exists at the time the audit is run.  
   • You may create the log however you want (parallel commands, find/xargs, etc.) but the final file must appear exactly as specified.  
   • The header row must be the very first line and must be spelled exactly “link_path<TAB>points_to<TAB>state”.  
   • There must be precisely four data lines (one per link). No extra blank lines at the end.  
5. Ensure all operations are performed without elevated privileges, and do not copy or move the original binaries or data—use symbolic links only.

Hints for multi-step parallelisation (do NOT output the actual commands here):  
• You might find “ln ‑s,” “find ‑L,” “parallel,” or “xargs ‑P” useful to create or validate links concurrently.  
• Remember that relative links can be generated with the help of “ln ‑sT” or by computing path prefixes dynamically.

The automated grader will:

A. Verify the directory structure and that every symbolic link is present, relative, and resolves to the correct target.  
B. Verify that /home/user/symlink_audit.log exists and matches the exact TSV format described above, including the header row, field order, TAB separators, and absence of extra blank lines.  
C. Confirm that every link listed in the log resolves to the same target shown in the points_to column and that the state column is “VALID”.  

Work entirely in the terminal; produce no extra files other than those explicitly required.
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem layout before the agent starts:

/home/user/apps/appAlpha-1.2/bin/alpha
  — a small text file with 2 lines:
      #!/bin/bash
      echo "Alpha v1.2"

/home/user/apps/appAlpha-1.2/perfdata/
  — empty directory

/home/user/apps/appBeta-2.0/bin/beta
  — a small text file with 2 lines:
      #!/bin/bash
      echo "Beta v2.0"

/home/user/apps/appBeta-2.0/perfdata/
  — empty directory

Items that MUST exist when the task is complete:

1. Directories
   /home/user/profile_workspace/
   /home/user/profile_workspace/alpha/
   /home/user/profile_workspace/beta/

2. Symbolic links (all relative)
   /home/user/profile_workspace/alpha/run   -> ../../apps/appAlpha-1.2/bin/alpha
   /home/user/profile_workspace/alpha/data  -> ../../apps/appAlpha-1.2/perfdata
   /home/user/profile_workspace/beta/run    -> ../../apps/appBeta-2.0/bin/beta
   /home/user/profile_workspace/beta/data   -> ../../apps/appBeta-2.0/perfdata

3. File
   /home/user/symlink_audit.log
   — exactly 5 lines:
     a. Header: link_path<TAB>points_to<TAB>state
     b-e. One line per link, each line with three TAB-separated fields, ending with literal string VALID.
     No trailing whitespace or blank lines.

Permissions:
All newly created directories: 755
All symlinks adopt default umask
The agent will not need root privileges.
